slides.title: The Modern Way 🏹
slides.content:
In addition to the traditional `function name() {}` syntax, modern JS/TS relies heavily on **Arrow Functions**.

These are sleeker, shorter, and handle the `this` keyword much better.

slides.title: Arrow Syntax
slides.content: Instead of the word `function`, you use an arrow `=>` between the parameters and the body block.

quizData.question: Which of these is the defining symbol of an arrow function?
quizData.explanation: Arrow functions utilize the 'fat arrow' `=>` syntax.

slides.title: Implicit Returns 💨
slides.content:
If your arrow function is extremely simple (just calculating to return a single line), you can omit the curly braces AND the `return` keyword!

This makes array operations like `.map()` and `.filter()` incredibly beautiful.
